title: hypericum linarioides subsp. alpestre
intro: Hypericum linarioides subsp. alpestre, also known as Alpine St. John's Wort, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the St. John's Wort family, characterized by its small yellow flowers and adaptability to alpine environments. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties. The health benefits associated with Alpine St. John's Wort include its potential to reduce anxiety and stress, improve sleep quality, and boost the immune system. Alpine St. John's Wort can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used to treat various health issues, including wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems.
